radiology.description: The leaders of the practice are formally or informally identified
The practice has a mission statement that reflects its strategic objectives and matches the needs of the community served
radiology.guideline: The mission statement is critical to the strategic planning process since it provides clear, guiding principles that define who the healthcare provider is as an organisation and why the practice exists. Mission statements create the foundation for action planning and a basis for accountability with the community.
radiology.guidelines: The mission statement is critical to the strategic planning process since it provides clear, guiding principles that define who the healthcare provider is as an organisation and why the practice exists. Mission statements create the foundation for action planning and a basis for accountability with the community.

radiology.description: The leaders are collectively responsible for ensuring that the mission statement is known to all personnel, patients, carers and the community served.
radiology.guideline: This can be achieved in many ways, such as by public display of the mission statement on notice boards, in the waiting area or included in printed information leaflets given to each patient when they first visit the practice. If the practice has a website, the mission statement can also be published on the website. Documented evidence that personnel have read the mission statement must be provided for full compliance with this criterion.

radiology.description: The practice participates in community health activities.
radiology.guideline: The mission of a practice should meet the needs of the community. To be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of interaction with members of the health committee and participation in community health activities.
radiology.guidelines: The mission of a practice should meet the needs of the community. To be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of interaction with members of the health committee and participation in community health activities.

radiology.description: The leaders, in liaison with the whole practice team, coordinate the compilation of an annual strategic plan and budget
radiology.guideline: This criterion requires all departments within the practice to be involved in the compilation of the annual strategic plan and budget to ensure that the needs of each department are discussed, considered and met if possible within the scope of the practice budget. Compliance will be demonstrated by documented evidence, for example, minutes of meetings.
radiology.guidelines: This criterion requires all departments within the practice to be involved in the compilation of the annual strategic plan and budget to ensure that the needs of each department are discussed, considered and met if possible within the scope of the practice budget. Compliance will be demonstrated by documented evidence, for example, minutes of meetings.

radiology.description: The leaders are collectively responsible for implementing the practice’s mission and strategic plan.
radiology.guideline: Evidence of an action plan for achieving the practice’s mission and strategic plan will be required.
radiology.guidelines: Evidence of an action plan for achieving the practice’s mission and strategic plan will be required.

radiology.description: The strategic plan is reviewed on an annual basis
radiology.guideline: Compliance will be measured by providing documented evidence that the practice’s strategic plan has been reviewed.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by providing documented evidence that the practice’s strategic plan has been reviewed.

radiology.description: Regular monitoring of the implementation of the strategic plan against envisaged timeframes and progress in achieving its objectives is documented at intervals determined by the practice
radiology.guideline: This criterion requires that practice leaders adhere to the timeframes that were set at the beginning of each year for achieving the strategic plan. For any plan to be implemented effectively, it is essential to monitor the progress that is being made towards achieving the outcomes that have been planned. This criterion further requires the practice to document the progress that is being made during the year towards achieving the strategic plan.

radiology.description: Where appropriate, the leadership roles in various positions are documented, agreed to and known by the personnel.
radiology.guideline: This may, for example include personnel responsible for monitoring infection control practices, resuscitation, stock control, patient files etc.
radiology.guidelines: This may, for example include personnel responsible for monitoring infection control practices, resuscitation, stock control, patient files etc.

radiology.description: The manager is responsible for the day-to-day running of the practice.
radiology.guideline: This criterion is assessed on evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC, the transgressions need to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC/NC rating and it should be based on accurate facts.
radiology.guidelines: This criterion is assessed on evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC, the transgressions need to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC/NC rating and it should be based on accurate facts.

radiology.description: The manager has the education and/or experience necessary to carry out his or her responsibilities.
radiology.guideline: If the practice manager is a solo practitioner or partner in the practice this criterion will be scored based on the evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If the practice manager is an employee of the practice, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
radiology.guidelines: If the practice manager is a solo practitioner or partner in the practice this criterion will be scored based on the evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If the practice manager is an employee of the practice, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

radiology.description: The practice is licensed in terms of relevant legislation for the level and type of services provided.
radiology.guideline: CRITICAL  Documented evidence is required.
radiology.guidelines: CRITICAL  Documented evidence is required.

radiology.description: The manager ensures that there is a system in place to monitor the implementation of applicable laws and regulations
radiology.guideline: This criterion will be scored compliant by default, although a PC rating is given whenever there is definite evidence of non-adherence to any legal requirement. In these cases, the transgression needs to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC rating and it should be based on accurate facts. Common examples of non-conformance with legal requirements include the following deficiencies: The required certificates are not available, for example, fire clearance certificates, electrical installation certificates, commissioning certificates (for example, ethylene oxide sterilisers), pressure test certificates for vessels under pressure Internal/external financial audits are not conducted Nurses transcribe doctors’ prescriptions and/or dispense medication Pharmaceutical items are incorrectly labelled Proof of current registration of professional personnel with the relevant councils is not available  In order to demonstrate compliance, practices may wish to compile a template listing the criteria requiring demonstration of compliance with country-specific legislation and regulations against the relevant national acts, regulations, etc. applicable to the criterion.

radiology.description: There is evidence of response to any reports from inspecting and regulatory authorities
The manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
radiology.guideline: The criterion will be scored after the final assessment of Management and Leadership criteria and will be scored according to the findings of financial and human resource management and other criteria dealing with adequate supply and effective management of resources (consumables, equipment etc.). radiology.description: The practice leaders facilitate communication between teams where relevant and between individual personnel members.
radiology.guideline: ROOT “Teams” refer to all the groups of employees and practitioners involved in the practice, for example administration, cleaning and clinical personnel. Minutes of personnel meetings, memos and communication tools can be provided as evidence. There must be evidence of at least quarterly personnel meetings.

Minutes of personnel meetings, memos and communication tools can be provided as evidence. There must be evidence of at least quarterly personnel meetings.

radiology.description: Agendas for meetings are prepared and shared in order to allow those attending to prepare for participation.
radiology.guideline: An assessment of a sample of agendas of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and “general” compliance as the criterion should not be penalised on one or two exceptions.
radiology.guidelines: An assessment of a sample of agendas of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and “general” compliance as the criterion should not be penalised on one or two exceptions.

radiology.description: Minutes of meetings are taken and are circulated to all relevant personnel.
radiology.guideline: An assessment of a sampled minutes of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and “general” compliance as the criterion should not be penalised for one or two exceptions. The manner in which minutes are circulated should be assessed and should consist of documented evidence of the circulation and acknowledgment of receipt.
radiology.guidelines: An assessment of a sampled minutes of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and “general” compliance as the criterion should not be penalised for one or two exceptions. The manner in which minutes are circulated should be assessed and should consist of documented evidence of the circulation and acknowledgment of receipt.

radiology.description: There is a procedure to make sure that important matters resulting from management meetings are communicated to and acted upon by personnel.
radiology.guideline: The existing “mechanism” should be assessed for effectiveness in conveying this information to personnel but surveyors must further note the importance of searching for evidence of “and acted upon”. This generally requires that minutes should reflect the allocation of responsibilities for carrying out tasks and that these are reported on at subsequent meetings.
radiology.guidelines: The existing “mechanism” should be assessed for effectiveness in conveying this information to personnel but surveyors must further note the importance of searching for evidence of “and acted upon”. This generally requires that minutes should reflect the allocation of responsibilities for carrying out tasks and that these are reported on at subsequent meetings.

radiology.description: The practice plans and coordinates comprehensive care by establishing relations and maintaining contact with other relevant services and agencies, including both governmental and nongovernmental agencies.
radiology.guideline: The purpose of this criterion is to ensure that practitioners are aware of members of the multidisciplinary team in both the private and government sector and engage them when required to ensure that the patient receives the best possible care. Documented evidence of such networking (for example, minutes of the meetings or e-mail correspondence) should be made available for assessment.

Documented evidence of such networking (for example, minutes of the meetings or e-mail correspondence) should be made available for assessment.

radiology.description: Information on services, hours of operation and processes for obtaining care is provided to services in the community who work in collaboration with the practice.
radiology.guideline: This information needs to be provided for example, to community nursing teams, step down care, allied health professionals etc. Compliance can be demonstrated by for example, e-mail correspondence with the various services or by providing information pamphlets about the practice to these services.
radiology.guidelines: This information needs to be provided for example, to community nursing teams, step down care, allied health professionals etc. Compliance can be demonstrated by for example, e-mail correspondence with the various services or by providing information pamphlets about the practice to these services.

radiology.description: The practice determines that the receiving individual/organisation can meet the patient’s continuing care needs, and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.
radiology.guideline: Documented evidence is required.
radiology.guidelines: Documented evidence is required.

radiology.description: Policies and procedures that guide and support the different services offered by the practice are implemented.
radiology.guideline: ROOT  The criterion will be scored at the end of the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC Surveyors must document examples of non-compliance. This criterion will automatically be scored NC if there is evidence of non-compliance with policies and procedures that affect patient and personnel safety.
radiology.guidelines: ROOT  The criterion will be scored at the end of the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC Surveyors must document examples of non-compliance. This criterion will automatically be scored NC if there is evidence of non-compliance with policies and procedures that affect patient and personnel safety.

radiology.description: Policies and procedures are signed and dated by persons authorised to do so.
radiology.guideline: The initial implementation date and the name, designation and signature of the person authorising the policy must be clearly legible on each policy and procedure.
radiology.guidelines: The initial implementation date and the name, designation and signature of the person authorising the policy must be clearly legible on each policy and procedure.

radiology.description: Policies and procedures are correctly compiled, indexed and filed.
radiology.guideline: • Title: Each policy should have a name or title for identification and reference. Identifying number: This is to facilitate indexing, locating the policy when required and communicating about the policy accurately. Policy statement: What is to be achieved and why. Procedure: How the policy is to be achieved. This is a step-by-step description of what needs to be done (like a recipe – following each step should result in a predictable outcome irrespective of who follows the steps). Associated references: This is required when the policy is directly associated with specific laws, regulations or policies or corporate policies and procedures. Dates: The date of the original policy, the date(s) of any revision(s) and the date of the next planned review. Signature: This is the signature of the person identified and authorised to approve policies and procedures If policies and procedures are only available electronically, all personnel must be able to demonstrate how to access the documents.

radiology.description: Policies and procedures are reviewed according to practice policy and then dated and signed.
radiology.guideline: Policies and procedures must be reviewed at least every 5 years and when changes in evidence based practice requires earlier review.
radiology.guidelines: Policies and procedures must be reviewed at least every 5 years and when changes in evidence based practice requires earlier review.

radiology.description: There is a process to ensure that personnel are familiar with relevant policies and procedures.
radiology.guideline: Compliance with this criterion can be achieved by means of, for example, signature sheets attached to each policy and procedure or by attendance registers at in-service training relating to policies and procedures.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion can be achieved by means of, for example, signature sheets attached to each policy and procedure or by attendance registers at in-service training relating to policies and procedures.

radiology.description: There are documented processes for staffing the practice.
radiology.guideline: Compliance with this criterion will require the practitioner to show documented evidence that patient load and patient acuity were studied and considered when planning the staffing for the practice. This must be evident for both the number of personnel required as well as their qualifications.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion will require the practitioner to show documented evidence that patient load and patient acuity were studied and considered when planning the staffing for the practice. This must be evident for both the number of personnel required as well as their qualifications.

radiology.description: The desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge are defined for personnel.
radiology.guideline: These requirements must be discussed and agreed by all relevant personnel during the recruitment process and form part of the position description.
radiology.guidelines: These requirements must be discussed and agreed by all relevant personnel during the recruitment process and form part of the position description.

radiology.description: The processes include measures to improve personnel retention.
There is implementation of a reliable, documented process for evaluating and verifying the credentials of all health professionals.
radiology.guideline: This can be achieved for example, by having a standardised checklist of requirements that must be verified. This should include reference checks. The completed document can then be retained in the employee’s personnel file. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of professional personnel.
radiology.guidelines: This can be achieved for example, by having a standardised checklist of requirements that must be verified. This should include reference checks. The completed document can then be retained in the employee’s personnel file. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of professional personnel.

radiology.description: Personnel files contain copies of qualifications and licences/registration from the relevant authority for all health professionals.
radiology.guideline: Copies of all the relevant original degrees/diplomas/certificates must be available, as well as evidence of registration with the relevant registration bodies. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files.
radiology.guidelines: Copies of all the relevant original degrees/diplomas/certificates must be available, as well as evidence of registration with the relevant registration bodies. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files.

radiology.description: There is a system to track the annual registration of all health professionals.
radiology.guideline: The system must reflect the date that each category of health professional’s licence is due for renewal and document that the original, renewed licence has been seen.
radiology.guidelines: The system must reflect the date that each category of health professional’s licence is due for renewal and document that the original, renewed licence has been seen.

radiology.description: All personnel with direct contact with the public have had a police check, a copy of which is kept in their personnel file.
radiology.guideline: Police checks are repeated every 3 years or as appropriate. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel file.
radiology.guidelines: Police checks are repeated every 3 years or as appropriate. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel file.

radiology.description: There are documented processes for orientation of personnel to the practice.
radiology.guideline: This includes general orientation and position-specific orientation as discussed in the standard intent above.
radiology.guidelines: This includes general orientation and position-specific orientation as discussed in the standard intent above.

radiology.description: The practice supports continuing education for its clinical personnel and maintains records of this in personnel files.
radiology.guideline: This refers specifically to professional personnel and the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies, where applicable. Management must have a clear strategy for assisting professional personnel to maintain their continued registration.
radiology.guidelines: This refers specifically to professional personnel and the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies, where applicable. Management must have a clear strategy for assisting professional personnel to maintain their continued registration.

radiology.description: There is a development strategy for the practice that ensures that the practice manager and administrative personnel receive the training required to fulfil their responsibilities.
radiology.guideline: This training could include billing, medical aid codes and triaging, (which is particularly important when administrative personnel are responsible for the initial assessment of patients in the waiting room).
radiology.guidelines: This training could include billing, medical aid codes and triaging, (which is particularly important when administrative personnel are responsible for the initial assessment of patients in the waiting room).

radiology.description: Personnel members are informed of opportunities to participate in advanced education, training, research, and other experiences.
radiology.guideline: Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of personnel or other training records. Personnel members may also be interviewed.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of personnel or other training records. Personnel members may also be interviewed.

radiology.description: A designated person is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of a financial strategy.
radiology.guideline: This requires that an individual in the practice has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of financial management. This may be the practice manager, a financial manager or a shared responsibility between practice manager and a financial officer.
radiology.guidelines: This requires that an individual in the practice has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of financial management. This may be the practice manager, a financial manager or a shared responsibility between practice manager and a financial officer.

radiology.description: The designated person is suitably qualified and/or experienced in accounting and financial management.
radiology.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
radiology.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

radiology.description: The responsibilities of this person include ensuring that policies and procedures for all financial functions are implemented.
radiology.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, policies and procedures must guide their functions.
radiology.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, policies and procedures must guide their functions.

radiology.description: There is a current budget for the practice.
radiology.guideline: For practices that comprise of more than one practitioner there should be an approved budget available in the management documentation. For practices that comprise of a solo practitioner there should be evidence of a practice budget. The budget must demonstrate allocation of resources. The budget will include, for example, salaries, rent, utility costs (water and electricity), consumable stock etc.
radiology.guidelines: For practices that comprise of more than one practitioner there should be an approved budget available in the management documentation. radiology.description: A report is produced at least annually, setting out the financial position to date.
radiology.guideline: This criterion requires that at least the most recent financial report that is prepared annually for submission to the relevant authorities such as Botswana Unified Revenue Service (BURS) must be available during the survey.
radiology.guidelines: This criterion requires that at least the most recent financial report that is prepared annually for submission to the relevant authorities such as Botswana Unified Revenue Service (BURS) must be available during the survey.

radiology.description: There is a mechanism for establishing the reason for budget variation in either income or expenditure.
radiology.guideline: Evidence should exist in the format in which financial statements are produced. Generally, these statements contain a column/section indicating the variance, i.e. under- or over-expenditure. Evidence showing what is done about this variance (especially over-expenditure), should be available, for example, in the minutes of relevant financial management meetings, personnel meetings or memos.

radiology.description: Capital investment proposals are subject to unanimous agreement among the partners or are agreed according to a voting system acceptable to all partners in the practice.
radiology.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored “not applicable” in practices owned by a solo practitioner.
radiology.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored “not applicable” in practices owned by a solo practitioner.

radiology.description: The practice has documented ethical and legal policies and procedures for the financial management of the practice that are implemented.
radiology.guideline: Examples of compliance with this criterion can be found in the policies and procedures for billing or referral to partners within the practice (for example partners in a poly clinic).
radiology.guidelines: Examples of compliance with this criterion can be found in the policies and procedures for billing or referral to partners within the practice (for example partners in a poly clinic).

radiology.description: Internal and external financial audit systems which meet audit requirements are maintained.
radiology.guideline: Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as Botswana Companies Act.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as Botswana Companies Act.

radiology.description: Annual audited financial statements are produced within the required time frame.
radiology.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as of the Botswana Companies Act.
radiology.guidelines: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as of the Botswana Companies Act.

radiology.description: There is a capital asset register, which is routinely maintained.
radiology.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.
radiology.guidelines: Documented evidence will be required.

radiology.description: Assets are insured.
radiology.guideline: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.
radiology.guidelines: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.

radiology.description: All health professionals provide evidence of professional indemnity insurance.
radiology.guideline: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.

radiology.description: An individual is designated to control the ordering, storage, distribution and control of equipment and supplies used in the practice.
radiology.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ equipment and supplies. Where a supply chain manager/stock controller is employed or the function is performed by someone other than the solo practice manager, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

radiology.description: Policies and procedures relating to all aspects of provisioning/supply chain management are implemented.
radiology.guideline: As a minimum, the policies and procedures discussed in a) – d) in the standard intent above should be available, accessible to personnel and implementation should be monitored.

radiology.description: Secure storage facilities are available.
radiology.guideline: The store must be lockable and exclude any unauthorised entry.
radiology.guidelines: The store must be lockable and exclude any unauthorised entry.

radiology.description: Prescription pads, letterheads, investigation request forms, administrative records and other official documents are accessible only to authorised persons.
radiology.guideline: Compliance will be verified by observation and personnel interviews.
radiology.guidelines: Compliance will be verified by observation and personnel interviews.

radiology.description: There are documented risk management processes for identifying all risks relating to practice processes and systems, personnel, patients, visitors to the practice and physical facilities.
radiology.guideline: Compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence that the practice personnel have collaboratively identified risks within the practice. This document will be used to develop an action plan to eliminate or minimise risk. The risk management process should include all relevant aspects and services of the practice, for example patient, personnel and visitor related risks; financial, corporate and legal risks; physical facility, security and environmental risks; etc. This does not necessarily require a single integrated document, provided all components are dealt with in documented systems for the relevant operational processes/functions/sections. (NOTE: doing only monthly workplace inspections does not qualify for a compliance rating).
radiology.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence that the practice personnel have collaboratively identified risks within the practice. This document will be used to develop an action plan to eliminate or minimise risk. The risk management process should include all relevant aspects and services of the practice, for example patient, personnel and visitor related risks; financial, corporate and legal risks; physical facility, security and environmental risks; etc. This does not necessarily require a single integrated document, provided all components are dealt with in documented systems for the relevant operational processes/functions/sections. radiology.description: The practice manager ensures the development and implementation of written policies and procedures for risk management processes and activities.
radiology.guideline: These policies and procedures must define the routine risk management processes, for example, monthly inspections to monitor risks, and the actions to be taken when risks materialise as adverse events.
radiology.guidelines: These policies and procedures must define the routine risk management processes, for example, monthly inspections to monitor risks, and the actions to be taken when risks materialise as adverse events.

radiology.description: A nominated individual with relevant qualifications, skills and/or experience supervises the implementation of the risk management programme.
radiology.guideline: In small practices, this will usually be the practice manager. Larger practices may have a designated risk manager.
radiology.guidelines: In small practices, this will usually be the practice manager. Larger practices may have a designated risk manager.

radiology.description: Ongoing in-service training of all personnel in risk management policies, procedures and principles is documented.
radiology.guideline: A record must be kept of such training and must include all personnel.
radiology.guidelines: A record must be kept of such training and must include all personnel.

radiology.description: Risk management systems are reviewed whenever there are changes in practice systems and processes or physical facilities.
radiology.guideline: This will include for example, during building renovations, changing from paper-based to electronic patient records or financial documentation, changing service providers etc.
radiology.guidelines: This will include for example, during building renovations, changing from paper-based to electronic patient records or financial documentation, changing service providers etc.

radiology.description: A nominated individual is responsible for infection control in the practice and practice personnel are aware of the nomination.
radiology.guideline: It is this nominated individual’s responsibility to ensure that infection control policies and procedures are implemented and monitored.
radiology.guidelines: It is this nominated individual’s responsibility to ensure that infection control policies and procedures are implemented and monitored.

radiology.description: Written policies and procedures guide personnel in the implementation of the infection control programme.
radiology.guideline: These policies and procedures must be available and accessible in the practice and their implementation monitored.
radiology.guidelines: These policies and procedures must be available and accessible in the practice and their implementation monitored.

radiology.description: Regular in-service training is given to all personnel in the field of infection control and is documented.
radiology.guideline: The infection control education programme needs to include policies/guidelines as well as relevant issues as they are identified. All personnel should be included in information sharing and training at regular scheduled meetings or other fora. Documented evidence (for example, minutes of meetings/attendance records) must be provided.
radiology.guidelines: The infection control education programme needs to include policies/guidelines as well as relevant issues as they are identified. All personnel should be included in information sharing and training at regular scheduled meetings or other fora. radiology.description: Infection control is on the agenda of all personnel meetings of the practice and discussion points are documented.

radiology.guideline: CRITICAL  This includes exposure to high risk infections and needle-stick injuries (for example body fluid splashes etc.) The national policy must be available and known to personnel.

radiology.description: There is a documented policy for the triage of patients with potential communicable diseases.
radiology.guideline: These patients are identified when requesting an appointment and appropriately triaged. Where there is no separate waiting area, the practitioner must attend to the patient immediately.

radiology.description: A mechanism known to the personnel is available for summoning the assistance of security/police/protection services in the case of an emergency.

radiology.description: Reasonable measures are taken to ensure the safety of lone workers.
radiology.guideline: This criterion is only applicable where practitioners consult with patients after hours. Reasonable measures can include for example, the installation of panic buttons or notifying another person or security company that the practitioner is alone in the practice. Sufficient arrangements should be in place to ensure that a personnel member who is alone in the practice is safe. These arrangements must be outlined in a policy which all personnel should be aware of.

radiology.description: There are structured systems and processes in place to ensure that all occupants of the practice’s facilities are safe from fire or smoke.

radiology.description: Documented certification is available from the relevant authority to show that the facility complies with applicable laws and regulations in relation to fire safety (fire clearance certificate).
radiology.guideline: CRITICAL  As per the intent statement, compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence of an official inspection to confirm that the building complies with national fire safety requirements. The practice manager must obtain a copy of the documentation from the landlord if the practice does not own the building.
radiology.guidelines: CRITICAL  As per the intent statement, compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence of an official inspection to confirm that the building complies with national fire safety requirements. radiology.description: Firefighting equipment is regularly inspected and serviced at least annually and the date of the service is recorded on the apparatus.
radiology.guideline: Guideline statement:  Abatement systems as per country specific requirements will be reflected in the certification as required in 1.7.5.2. It is essential that the testing and servicing of all fire safety equipment is up to date, automatic abatement systems are regularly tested, fire and smoke detection systems are tested, and automatic abatement doors are not forced to remain open by means of wedging or putting objects against them. Documented evidence of inspection, testing and maintenance of fire safety equipment is required. This may include the fixing of service labels onto the equipment itself.

radiology.description: Flammable materials are clearly labelled and safely stored.
Sufficient electrical socket outlets are provided in all areas to avoid overloading of individual outlets and to minimise fire risks.
Easily recognised and understood signs prohibiting smoking are displayed in areas where flammable materials and combustible gases are stored.
radiology.guideline: Compliance will be assessed during the assessment of the areas where such materials are stored.

radiology.description: Annual personnel training in fire prevention and evacuation procedures is documented.

radiology.description: There are site and floor plans that depict the locations and layout of the main services (for example, water, sanitation, electricity supply).

radiology.description: First aid kits and materials for healthcare workers are available.
radiology.guideline: Botswana specific occupational health laws and regulations should dictate the contents of the first aid kits. Please note that doctors may stock the required content as part of their regular stock. Other healthcare service providers such as, for example, physiotherapists and radiographers will need to have a dedicated first aid kit. Compliance will be measured on the availability of the required stock.

radiology.description: There is a documented policy for collection, collation, validation and distribution of data which is implemented.

radiology.description: Clinical, managerial and administrative personnel participate in developing and implementing an information system to support patient care and practice management.
radiology.guideline: This does not have to be a single system nor does it have to be electronic. Paper-based information system/s are also acceptable. The information required by the criteria must encompass at least information relating to individual patients, care provided, results of care and the practice’s own performance.

radiology.description: Clinical and managerial data and information are integrated as needed to support decision-making.
radiology.guideline: Assessment of compliance is based on the availability of reports on the various types of data as well as documented evidence (for example, minutes of meetings or other form of communication) of discussions on the information and how this is taken into consideration to assist with decision-making processes. This needs to happen at all levels of the practice and not just at management level, as information also needs to be available on departmental operations such as personnel matters, financial aspects, supply management, outcomes of quality improvement programmes, results from clinical and patient record audits, negative incidents, etc.

radiology.description: Security and confidentiality of data and information is maintained.

Confidentiality aspects need to be documented as part of the information management processes for the various types of information, whether it be financial data, personal information on personnel, patient information, etc. Where applicable, country-specific legal requirements need to be considered.

The training and experience required will depend on the level of care provided by the practice and the complexity of the information systems for which the individual is responsible. In some practices this may be the practice manager of the solo practitioner, in other practices a quality manager may be appointed.

Whatever format exists, the intention is that the “processes” should be comprehensive and reflect all components of quality management and improvement in the practice.

Measurement of these indicators will provide information on current performance and evidence of change over time following the implementation of quality improvement plans.

radiology.description: The practice holds regular meetings to discuss significant clinical issues.
radiology.guideline: Evidence of significant clinical issues can be determined from the incident management processes and during patient record audits and personnel interviews. Minutes of meetings and attendance records must be provided as evidence of compliance with this criterion.
radiology.guidelines: Evidence of significant clinical issues can be determined from the incident management processes and during patient record audits and personnel interviews. Minutes of meetings and attendance records must be provided as evidence of compliance with this criterion.

radiology.description: Information from a validated patient/family satisfaction audit tool is used to improve the quality of service delivery.
radiology.guideline: This requires that the practice does more than just provide information about the complaints process. Formal patient/family satisfaction surveys must be conducted regularly using an audit tool that is specifically designed to elicit information about the practice and the services that it offers. The tools used to measure patient feedback need to be rigorous and include all areas of the practice (for example waiting times, attitude of personnel, cleanliness of toilets, communication with clinical personnel, education etc.) to ensure the integrity of data subsequently used by practices for quality improvement purposes. The practice must decide on the method, frequency and number of patients to be surveyed but compliance with the criterion requires that it is representative of the size of the practice and the services offered. The practice needs to further provide documented evidence that the information from the survey was used to improve the quality of service delivery.

radiology.description: A documented procedure for the monitoring of near misses/incidents/adverse (sentinel) events is available, which includes the documentation of interventions, responses and corrective actions taken to recorded incidents.
radiology.guideline: A policy and procedure should be available to all personnel detailing the steps to follow in the event of such an incident occurring. This includes the reporting, recording and investigation of the event as well as the response to the event and the actions taken to prevent recurrence of the event, or minimise harm should the event recur. Personnel will be interviewed to confirm that they are familiar with the contents of these documents.

Personnel will be interviewed to confirm that they are familiar with the contents of these documents.

radiology.description: Formal significant event analyses are undertaken when necessary.
radiology.guideline: The practice manager/risk manager must identify those events where formal event analyses must be conducted as required by law, regulation and practice policy. All events that meet the definition must be assessed by performing a credible root cause analysis. When the root cause analysis reveals that systems improvement or other actions can prevent or reduce the risk of such events recurring, appropriate action must be taken to achieve this risk reduction.

radiology.description: Patient records are reviewed regularly and results analysed as part of the quality improvement process.

radiology.description: Patient privacy is protected when providing personal information
radiology.guideline: This must be evident in all patient care areas including administrative areas and reception areas. Patient privacy should be safeguarded at all times, including when providing personal information to nonclinical personnel. It is not always necessary to provide privacy by means of physical barriers such as walls and doors. Distance between waiting patients and those speaking with practice personnel can also provide the required privacy when less sensitive information is being exchanged. For example, a line can be drawn on the floor some distance away from the registration desk.  Background noise, e.g. music or patient education videos, can help to prevent conversations with patients being overheard.

radiology.description: The responsibilities of this person include maintaining quality control programmes.
radiology.guideline: The initial commissioning tests should be done by the supplier. radiology.description: There is a documented policy for reporting, investigating, and responding to patient care mismatching events when they occur and implementing changes, where relevant, to reduce the risk of unnecessary radiation exposure to patients.
Personnel receive education with regard to new procedures, equipment and newly-acquired hazardous materials.

radiology.description: Personal dosimeters worn by personnel comply with the ionising radiation regulations.
radiology.guideline: CRITICAL  Dosimeter badges should be available for all radiation workers. The register for radiation workers needs to be available and up to date. Every registered radiation worker should be supplied with a dosimeter. This dosimeter must be replaced every 32 days, unless a radiation occurrence is suspected or has been established. All full-time theatre personnel must be monitored. In cases where the workload is very low e.g. every three to four months, these staff members may be exempted from being monitored. A file should be available with dosimeter reading reports for all registered persons.

radiology.description: Signs warning of the dangers of radiation to pregnant and breastfeeding women are prominently displayed.
radiology.guideline: Although every female patient of reproductive age should have the date of her last menstrual period recorded on the request form and also asked of the possibility of pregnancy prior to high-risk procedures in order to avert tragedy, these signs will serve as an additional prompt for patients who may be pregnant to alert personnel in the diagnostic imaging service of this fact.
